question: Will CPI rise more than 0.8% in March 2026?
reasoning: The March 2026 CPI data has already been released by the Bureau of Labor Statistics on April 10, 2026. The Consumer Price Index increased only 0.3% in March 2026 on a seasonally adjusted basis, which is significantly below the 0.8% threshold required for YES resolution. This means the market should resolve to NO with 100% certainty, making the current 65% price dramatically overvalued.
key_factors: March 2026 CPI already released showing 0.3% increase
risks: Potential data revision (though highly unlikely for such a large change)
